name: Loanmax Title Loans
description_short: LoanMax Title Loans, founded in 1990, offers auto title loans up to $10,000 at multiple Las Vegas-area branches. Vehicle, photo ID, and clear title required.
description_long:
LoanMax Title Loans was founded in 1990 in Jonesboro, Georgia, and has grown into one of the more established names in the auto title lending industry. The company describes itself as helping "hardworking Americans use the equity in their vehicle to access the cash they need." With over 35 years of operation, LoanMax maintains a multi-state presence with documented licensing in Nevada, Virginia, Delaware, South Carolina, New Mexico, and Michigan. Corporate management runs through Select Management Resources, headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia. In Nevada, the company operates at least five Las Vegas-area branches, making it one of the more geographically accessible title lenders in the market.

LoanMax provides cash loans secured by a borrower's vehicle title, with loan amounts reaching up to $10,000 subject to vehicle evaluation and the applicant's ability to repay. The process requires three items: the vehicle itself, a government-issued photo ID, and a clear (lien-free) vehicle title. Borrowers retain use of their vehicle while the loan is outstanding. Repayment options include cash, personal check, debit card, and Western Union. An online payment portal is also available. Las Vegas locations are open Monday through Friday from 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM and on Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM.

LoanMax's primary differentiators are its operational longevity and multi-state regulatory compliance infrastructure. The company publishes state-specific licensing documentation and consumer disclosures for Nevada, New Mexico, South Carolina, Delaware, Virginia, and Michigan — a level of regulatory transparency not universal among title lenders. For Nevada borrowers specifically, LoanMax includes a state-mandated disclosure that title loans are intended for short-term needs only and that customers with credit difficulties should seek credit counseling before proceeding. The five Las Vegas metro locations reduce friction for borrowers who need in-person service.

Auto title loans carry meaningful financial risk, and LoanMax's website does not disclose interest rates, APR, fees, or repayment terms — information that is essential before pledging a vehicle as collateral. Title loans as a product category typically carry triple-digit APRs and short repayment windows; defaulting can result in vehicle repossession. Nevada's own regulatory disclosure, which LoanMax is required to display, explicitly warns borrowers this is not a long-term financial solution. Prospective borrowers should exhaust lower-cost alternatives — credit union payday alternative loans (PALs), community development financial institution (CDFI) products, or nonprofit credit counseling — before proceeding with a title loan.
address: 2295 E Tropicana Ave, Las Vegas, NV 89119

company_info.headquarters: 2295 E Tropicana Ave, Las Vegas, NV 89119

pros: Founded in 1990 — over 35 years of operating history in the title loan industry
Loans available up to $10,000 based on vehicle evaluation
Minimal documentation required: vehicle, photo ID, and clear title only
Five Las Vegas metro locations provide convenient in-person access
Accepts multiple payment methods: cash, check, debit card, and Western Union
Online payment portal available for loan repayments
Publishes state-specific regulatory disclosures and license documentation for multiple states
cons: No interest rates, APR, or fee schedules disclosed anywhere on the website
Vehicle repossession is a real consequence of non-payment — your car is the collateral
Nevada state-mandated warning on the site explicitly flags these as short-term only and unsuitable for ongoing credit needs
No Sunday hours; limited Saturday hours (9 AM–2 PM only)
Loan approval is subject to vehicle evaluation with no published criteria or minimum amounts
best_for: Vehicle owners with a clear, lien-free title who need fast access to cash up to $10,000
Borrowers with poor or no credit history who cannot qualify for conventional personal loans
Las Vegas-area residents who prefer or require in-person loan processing
People facing a one-time, short-term cash shortfall who have a repayment plan in place
diagnosis: LoanMax is best suited for Las Vegas-area vehicle owners with a clear title who need a lump sum up to $10,000 quickly and cannot qualify for lower-cost credit products. The critical caveat is that APR and fee terms are entirely absent from the website, title loans are a high-cost product category, and non-payment puts your vehicle at direct risk of repossession — borrowers should treat this as a last resort and have a concrete repayment plan before applying.
services: Auto title loans secured by vehicle equity
In-person loan processing at multiple Las Vegas locations
Multi-location Las Vegas metro network (5 branches)
